pytorch.reason: not used: this probe isolates networkx graph algorithm capability; pytorch tensor operations are addressed in dedicated integration sims per four-sim-kinds doctrine

pyg.reason: not used: this probe isolates networkx graph algorithm capability; PyG message-passing neural networks are a separate concern addressed in integration sims per four-sim-kinds doctrine

z3.reason: not used: this probe isolates networkx graph algorithm capability; SMT constraint verification over graph properties deferred to integration sims per four-sim-kinds doctrine

cvc5.reason: not used: this probe isolates networkx graph algorithm capability; cvc5 formal proof checking deferred to integration sims per four-sim-kinds doctrine

sympy.reason: not used: this probe isolates networkx graph algorithm capability; symbolic verification of graph invariants deferred to integration sims per four-sim-kinds doctrine

clifford.reason: not used: this probe isolates networkx graph algorithm capability; geometric algebra over graph structures deferred to integration sims per four-sim-kinds doctrine

geomstats.reason: not used: this probe isolates networkx graph algorithm capability; Riemannian geometry embedding deferred to integration sims per four-sim-kinds doctrine

e3nn.reason: not used: this probe isolates networkx graph algorithm capability; equivariant spherical harmonic networks deferred to integration sims per four-sim-kinds doctrine

rustworkx.reason: not used: this probe isolates networkx graph algorithm capability; rustworkx cross-validation is the focus of the dedicated crosscheck integration sim

xgi.reason: not used: this probe isolates networkx graph algorithm capability; hyperedge structures handled by xgi are out of scope for this isolation probe

toponetx.reason: not used: this probe isolates networkx graph algorithm capability; topological complex operations are out of scope for this isolation probe

gudhi.reason: not used: this probe isolates networkx graph algorithm capability; persistent homology analysis deferred to integration sims per four-sim-kinds doctrine

target_tool.name: networkx
can: rich graph algorithm library covering paths, centrality, clustering, flows, matching
interoperates with scipy sparse arrays and numpy for numeric analysis
directed and undirected graphs with arbitrary node and edge attributes
DAG detection, topological sort, and ancestor/descendant queries
cannot: GPU acceleration — slower than rustworkx or igraph for large graphs
hyperedges or higher-order structures (use xgi or toponetx for those)
formal constraint satisfaction over graph properties (use z3 for that)
